Suffrage association. The suf-


fragets organization is the Ma
tional Progressive .Women's Suf-
frage union. There are also num-
erOus smaller societies.


In New York are also the head
quarters of the International
Woman's Suffrage alliance, Of
which Mrs. Carrie Chapman Gait
Is president, and which has


branches in 90 countries. The anti
suffragist association is the Ma
tional League for the Civic Edu-


cation of Women, and Mrs. Rich
ard Watson Glider is president,


Mrs. Gilbert e. Jones chairman,


Mrs. Humphrey Ward vice presi-


dent, Mrs. Andrew Carnegie and


Mrs. Grover Cleveland honorary


vice presidents, and Henry Clews,


Rev. Lyman Abbott and Rev. c. H.


Parhurst members of the ad-
visory board. That the Ameri-
can movement will eventually be-


come more like the militant Eng


lish movement, is the belief of


many women. Mrs. Belmont, who


has given $4,000 to the cause,
favors the militant movement, as


does Lady Cook Tennessee Olaf
linz. who has promised a million
to the cause.


The suffragists and the suffra-


gets conduct an active campaign


all the time. Helen Murphy says
